description_long: This is a traditional banjo with a circular resonator body and a long, slender neck. The body features a drum-like membrane stretched over a round frame, with visible tension hooks around the perimeter. The neck is made of light-colored wood and has evenly spaced frets, with four tuning pegs on the headstock and one side peg, indicating a five-string configuration. The bridge and tailpiece are metallic, and the headstock is simple, lacking elaborate ornamentation. The overall finish is matte, with a natural wood grain visible on the neck and a white drumhead on the body.
description: A classic five-string banjo with a round drum body, light wood neck, and simple headstock, featuring metal hardware and a white membrane.
description_view_0: The back of the banjo is visible, showing the round body, the neck, and the tuning pegs on the headstock.
description_view_1: A side view highlights the depth of the drum body, the tension hooks, and the length of the neck.
description_view_2: The front view displays the white drumhead, bridge, tailpiece, and the full fretboard with tuning pegs.
description_view_3: The opposite side view shows the body depth and the neck's attachment to the body, with tuning pegs partially visible.
attribute_headstock_design: Simple rectangular headstock with four tuning pegs on the back and one side peg, no visible logo or elaborate contours
